description: Machine-readable launch policy controlling worker scheduling based on main branch health and lock-aware conflict resolution.

green.label: Healthy
green.meaning: Main is healthy. All worker types may launch.

yellow.label: Degraded
yellow.gateResult: Non-critical failure (test env flake, boundary guard warning)
yellow.meaning: Main has a known issue that does not block source compilation. Limited worker types may launch.

red.label: Broken
red.gateResult: Critical failure (build broken, type-check fails, Prisma schema invalid)
red.meaning: Main is broken. Only recovery workers may launch.

black.label: Unrecoverable
black.meaning: Main is in an unrecoverable state. No automated workers may launch.

classification.explicitField: mainHealthPolicy = gate-all AND risk = high AND allowedFiles in src/prisma|src/generated|src/database
classification.heuristic: allowedFiles includes src/ AND risk = high

classification.explicitField: mainHealthPolicy = gate-all AND allowedFiles in scripts/

test-only.description: Add or fix tests, no source change

launchPermissionMatrix.description: Which worker types may launch in each health state.

rationale.runtime-feature: Requires green main because changes must compile and pass tests on a healthy base.
rationale.foundation-fix: Allowed in all non-black states to repair broken state.
rationale.docs: Allowed in yellow because docs cannot worsen a build failure.
rationale.health-repair: Always allowed (except black) — blocking would create deadlock where main stays broken.
rationale.test-only: Blocked in red because test failures may mask the real breakage.

conflictGroups.enforcement: Tasks in the same conflict group MUST NOT run in parallel.
conflictGroups.docsExemption: Tasks whose allowedFiles are exclusively under docs/ are exempt from duplicate-group rejection.

auth-core.reason: Serial chain, each builds on the previous

posts.reason: P2-P4 depend on P1, share posts.service.ts

sharedLocks.description: Fine-grained locks on common resources. Tasks claiming the same lock cannot run in parallel.

package.rule: Single-writer: only one task may modify package files at a time.

prisma-schema.rule: Single-writer: only one task may modify Prisma schema at a time.

app-module.rule: Single-writer: NestJS module imports are a flat list with positional merge semantics. Two workers wiring different modules produce last-write-wins conflict.

docs-index.rule: Low-conflict: docs-only tasks may share this lock if no file overlap.

timeoutDefaults.description: Default budget values by worker type. Task JSON may override.

dependencyFacts.description: Default blockedBy chains by worker type. Task JSON may override.

postMergeStopConditions.description: Actions the orchestrator MUST take when health gate fails after a merge.
steps: Classify the failure using post-merge-health-gate categories.
Set health state (yellow or red) based on classification.
Cancel or defer in-flight workers whose type is not permitted in the current state.
Block new launches for disallowed worker types until health recovers.
Dispatch a recovery worker if failure is red-state.
Re-run health gate after recovery PR merges.
Resume normal launches only when state returns to green (or yellow for permitted types).

inFlightHandling.prNotOpened: Abort the worker. Comment on the issue explaining the health stop.
inFlightHandling.prOpenNotReviewed: Hold the PR. Do not request review until main recovers. Re-validate after recovery.
inFlightHandling.prApprovedNotMerged: Block merge. Re-validate after main recovers.
inFlightHandling.prMergedCausedFailure: The merged PR is the recovery target. Launch a revert or fix worker.

blackStateRules.description: Black state is unrecoverable by automation. All workers blocked. Requires manual intervention.
blackStateRules.escalation: Notify repo owner. No automated recovery path.
